pages.raw_text:
OLYMPIA, Aug. pa. w. Per-
ley, inspector of tracks and safety
appliances for the railroad commits
sion, has filed report in which he
complains about the practice Of
the Northern Pacific railroad of
hauling bad order cars, even after
receiving notification not to do so.


In his report Inspector Perley
says that on July chained-up
cars were hauled out Of Seattle, the
train being in charge of Conductor
Mckinley and Engineer Reed. Aft
er this train had pulled out, Inspec-
tor Perley says he notified the
master mechanic, w. B. Norton, of
the practice of hauling bad order
cars contrary to the law, but in
spite of that notification the same
crew was sent out on July with a
train In which there were 10 chain
ed-up cars, Gin defiance" says the
inspector, "of my protest"


Inspector Periey also gives a list
of 13 bad order cars hauled from
Pasco to Ellensburg on Aug. 4, and
II bad order cars hauled out of Ell
lensburg on Aug. 6. Car foremen
and master mechanics have been
notified by Inspector Perley of
these movements of bad order cars.
